title: Easy DIY Lazy Susan
summary: These plans will show you exactly how to make a custom rotating serving board (lazy Susan) using quality hardwood.
description: These plans will show you exactly how to make a custom rotating serving board (lazy Susan) using quality hardwood. A step-by-step guide from rough lumber to finished product.

materials.name: Hardwood of Choice (Maple, Walnut, Cherry), 8'x 4" x 0.75" 8' board
Bottom base piece, 8" x 8" 8" x 8" board

steps.title: Cutting Pieces
steps.body: The first step is to cut the pieces to the proper size. (I made my board 16") however you can customize the size you want to cut. The KREG Trim Router Circle Guide can cut circles from 3.5" to 52" Repeat until you have 4 x16" pieces.

steps.title: Glue the pieces together
steps.body: Next, you glue the boards together to form the piece that the circle will be cut out of. Use KREG clamps that fit your piece.

steps.title: Cutting the Circle
steps.body: In this step, use the KREG Trim Router Circle Guide to cut the desired size circle. Follow directions properly.

steps.title: Clean the circle
steps.body: Using the KREG random orbit sander, clean up the circle and sand using a variety of sandpaper grits from 80-220 grit. Optional: Use a round over bit in the KREG trim router to trim the edges around the circle.

steps.title: Attach the lazy Susan hardware
steps.body: Attach the lazy Susan hardware to both the pieces connecting them. (follow directions according to the hardware you use) A hole must be drilled in the bottom base piece to allow screws through to attach the hardware.

steps.title: Apply wood finish to the finished piece
steps.body:
Apply wood finish of your choice to the final product.

**Tip:** Selecting the Proper Finish It's important to select the proper finish for your piece. If you plan on using it for food directly, the wood finish must be a food safe mineral oil type of finish.
